model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Iowa State is the vastly superior team as a 2-seed with elite metrics and Big 12 experience, while Tennessee State is a 15-seed from the Ohio Valley Conference making its first tournament appearance since 1994. The Cyclones' defensive intensity and tournament-tested roster should overwhelm the Tigers, who have not faced a team of Iowa State's caliber all season. Historical 2 vs 15 matchups overwhelmingly favor the higher seed, and the massive talent disparity makes an upset extremely unlikely.

reasoningStep.title: Iowa State vs Tennessee State Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluating the 2/15 matchup between Iowa State (Big 12) and Tennessee State (OVC) in the Midwest Region.
evidence: Iowa State is a 2-seed with a strong Big 12 pedigree and elite defensive metrics under coach TJ Otzelberger.
Tennessee State is a 15-seed making its first NCAA Tournament appearance since 1994, with a 23-9 record from the Ohio Valley Conference.
Historical data shows 2-seeds have a 98.5% win rate against 15-seeds in the NCAA Tournament.
Iowa State's schedule strength and tournament experience create a massive talent gap that Tennessee State cannot overcome.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Tennessee State March Madness opponent is Iowa State in 2026 NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Sports Titans Predators Nashville SC Vanderbilt UT High Schools Other Colleges Sports Columnist Joe Rexrode NFL Draft Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA TSU basketball’s March Madness opponent is Iowa State to open NCAA tournament bracket Mike Organ Nashville Tennessean Show Caption Hide Caption Tennessee State's basketball reacts to hearing name on Selection Show Tennessee State's basketball players, surrounded by fans at Gentry Center, cheered after hearing they will face Iowa State in the NCAA Tournament. Tennessee State will play Iowa State in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. TSU secured its spot by winning the Ohio Valley Conference Tournament against Morehead State. This is the Tigers' first NCAA Tournament appearance since the 1993-94 season. Tennessee State basketball will face Iowa State in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. The Tigers (23-9) earned an automatic berth in March Madness by winning the Ohio Valley Conference Tournament . TSU defeated Morehead State 93-67 after earning a double-bye into the conference tournament by winning the regular season championship. Iowa State (27-7) is the No. 2 seed in the Midwest Region. TSU (23-9) is the No. 15 seed. They will play on March 20 (1:50 p.m. CT, CBS) in St. Louis. BUY TSU BASKETBALL TICKETS FOR MARCH MADNESS This is TSU's first time in the NCAA Tournament since 1993-94 and only its third time since becoming an NCAA Division I program. In his first year as Tennessee State's coach, Nolan Smith was the OVC Co-Coach of the Year after leading the Tigers to their best regular season record as an NCAA Division I team. TSU heads into March Madness on a six-game win streak, its longest since the 2017-18 season. The Tigers have won nine of their last 10 games.
